refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall update the worksite model within the customer-required timeliness for operational use by minimizing data acquisition latency and processing latency.
refined_fr.rationale: To meet the strict latency requirement (p95 ≤ 1.0 s), the overall update process is decomposed into data acquisition and data processing sub-functions, enabling targeted design and verification of each latency contributor.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure and verify the latency of data acquisition from sensors and telemetry, and separately measure the processing time to update the worksite model, ensuring combined latency meets the p95 ≤ 1.0 s criterion.
sub_frs: FR1: Acquire worksite data with minimal latency
FR2: Process and update worksite model with minimal latency

candidate_dps: DP1: Use high-frequency telemetry data streaming with prioritized network protocols
DP2: Implement real-time data processing algorithms optimized for low-latency updates
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP1 and DP2 combined to address acquisition and processing latency respectively

potential_sources: Tight coupling between telemetry data acquisition and processing timing
Dependency on network link performance affecting both DPs
coupling_check.notes: The DPs are logically coupled but can be modularized with clear interface boundaries to minimize cross-domain interference.

assumptions_risks: Assumes network link supports required bandwidth and low jitter
Risk of telemetry data loss or delay impacting processing timeliness
Processing algorithms must be optimized for worst-case data loads
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Monitor network performance and implement fallback or buffering strategies to mitigate telemetry delays.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: Overall system responsible for telemetry data acquisition, processing, and worksite model update

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Receives high-frequency telemetry data from Vehicle and Teleoperation modules

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Supports data transmission between Vehicle, Teleoperation, and NGHE Solution modules

module_containment_scores.notes: Centralizes telemetry data acquisition and processing, minimizing diffusion and maximizing containment.
Primarily data source; limited containment of update logic.
Involved in data generation and control but not in processing.
Not involved in worksite model update latency.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Centralizing the update latency functionality within the NGHE Solution module reduces interface complexity and latency diffusion.
containment_risks: Potential bottleneck if NGHE Solution module is overloaded
Dependency on network link reliability may affect latency
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Consider load balancing or distributed processing if latency targets are not met.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The refined FRs are clearly decomposed and mapped to logical DPs addressing acquisition and processing latency, with verification approaches defined.

diffusion_risks: Telemetry data dependency on network link may cause latency diffusion
Processing logic centralized in NGHE Solution may create a single point of failure
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Containment scores favor NGHE Solution as the primary module, minimizing diffusion and enabling clear ownership.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification can be performed by measuring latency end-to-end through telemetry interface and processing logs within NGHE Solution.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Allocation of FRs to NGHE Solution module is clear and aligns with stakeholder expectations and system architecture.

recommended_revisions: Monitor network link performance and consider fallback mechanisms
Evaluate potential for distributed processing if latency targets are not consistently met

decision.justification: The current level of functional decomposition, DP selection, and IM assignment satisfy the latency requirement with clear ownership and verification paths. No immediate need to zigzag or revise at this level.
next_level_sub_frs: FR1: Acquire worksite data with minimal latency
FR2: Process and update worksite model with minimal latency

revise.why: Current decomposition and assignments are adequate; future levels will further detail sub-FRs.

assumptions: Network link supports required bandwidth and latency
Telemetry data is reliable and timely
open_risks: Processing overload in NGHE Solution module
evidence_needed: Latency measurements from telemetry data acquisition
Processing time benchmarks for worksite model updates
